notes: REAM-merge of Qwen3-Coder-30B base. Matches opencode result (1/5=20%) — uniform degradation across BOTH scaffolds vs un-REAM coder-30b-eval (2/5 on same 5 = 40%). NOT a scaffold issue: REAM merge appears to have degraded codegen quality by ~50% relative on this small subset. Suggests R9700 team should revisit REAM recipe for coder bases, or that REAM is better suited to thinking-mode models than coder-tuned ones.
